pages.text: Normally the world builds itself a chunk at a time, as you walk into fresh ground. In a pack this large, generating new terrain is the single heaviest thing the game does — it is where most frontier stutter comes from.$(br2)$(italic)Chunky$() lets you do that work in advance.
pages.title: Pre-Generating the World

pages.text: $(bold)Chunky runs from the chat console.$() The four commands you need:$(br)$(li)$(italic)/chunky radius <blocks>$() — how far out to build from the centre$(li)$(italic)/chunky start$() — begin generating$(li)$(italic)/chunky pause$() — stop for now$(li)$(italic)/chunky continue$() — pick up where you left off$(br2)Unless you set one, the centre is wherever you are standing.
pages.title: The Commands

pages.text: $(bold)While it runs.$() Pre-generation is meant to be heavy — the game will run warm and a large radius takes time. You can keep playing meanwhile.$(br2)One thing to know: Chunky does $(bold)not$() resume on its own. Closing the game stops the task, and you must run $(italic)/chunky continue$() yourself next session. For a new world, a few hundred blocks around spawn is plenty.
pages.title: While It Runs
